games.title: Metroid (NES)
games.description: Metroid is a 1986 action-adventure game developed and published by Nintendo. Set on the planet Zebes, the story follows Samus Aran as she attempts to retrieve the predatory Metroid organisms that were stolen by Space Pirates, who plan to replicate the Metroids by exposing them to beta rays and then use them as biological weapons to destroy Samus and all who oppose them. It pioneered the Metroidvania genre, focusing on exploration and searching for power-ups used to reach previously inaccessible areas. Its varied endings for fast completion times made it an early popular title for speedrunning. It was also lauded for being one of the first video games to showcase a female protagonist.

games.title: Duck Hunt
games.description: Duck Hunt is a 1984 light gun shooter video game developed by Nintendo for the NES. It's a first-person shooter game with moving on-screen targets, firing the NES Zapper light gun at a CRT television screen. The player must hit a minimum number of targets to advance to the next round or else get a game over.

games.title: Zombies ate my Neighbors
games.description: Zombies Ate My Neighbors is a 1993 run and gun video game developed by LucasArts and published by Konami for the Super Nintendo Entertainment System. The player controls the protagonists Zeke and Julie in order to rescue the titular neighbors from monsters often seen in horror films. Aiding them in this task are a variety of weapons and power-ups that can be used to battle the numerous enemies in each level.

games.title: EVO Search for Eden
games.description: E.V.O.: Search for Eden is a 1992 action-adventure game developed by Almanic Corporation and published by Enix for the SNES. E.V.O.: Search for Eden involves the player navigating a creature through a number of side-scrolling levels while undergoing bodily evolution to cope with ever-changing environments. It is heavily based on Almanic's original title, 46 Okunen Monogatari ~The Shinka Ron~

games.title: Rocket Knight Adventures
games.description: Rocket Knight Adventures is a 1993 singleplayer platformer game. It was developed and published by Konami. You play as Sparkster, a sword-wielding possum knight with a rocket pack on his back. An army of robotic pigs has invaded the kingdom and kidnapped the princess; You must battle through seven stages to stop the Devotindos Empire. You can use the rocket pack to fly in eight directions and bounce off walls.

games.title: Comix Zone
games.description: Comix Zone is a 1995 beat 'em up game developed by Sega Technical Institute. It follows the comic writer/artist Sketch Turner who, after being transported into the comic by one of his creations, attempts to escape while contending with various threats. The game sees the player traversing six levels, where they interact with the comic world and must defeat enemies, or occasionally solve puzzles, to progress.

games.title: ECCO the Dolphin
games.description: Ecco the Dolphin is an action-adventure game developed by Ed Annunziata and Novotrade International and published by Sega for the Mega Drive/Genesis in 1992. The player character is a bottlenose dolphin who travels through time to combat hostile extraterrestrials in Earth's oceans and on an alien spacecraft.

games.title: Pokemon Red & Blue
games.description: Pokémon Red, Pokémon Blue and Pokémon Yellow are 1996 role-playing video games developed by Game Freak and published by Nintendo for the Game Boy. They are the first installments of the Pokémon video game series. The player controls the protagonist from an overhead perspective and navigates the fictional region of Kanto in a quest to master Pokémon battling. The goal is to become the Pokémon League Champion by defeating the eight Gym Leaders and the best Pokémon Trainers in the Kanto Region, the Elite Four. Another objective is to complete the Pokédex, an in-game encyclopedia, by obtaining all 151 Pokémon. Red and Blue use the Game Link Cable, which connects two Game Boy systems and allows Pokémon to be traded or battled between games.

games.title: F-Zero X
games.description: F-Zero X is a 1998 single & multiplayer racing game. It takes place in a futuristic world where pilots compete in high-speed antigravity races. You race against other characters and features 30 vehicles and 24 racetracks.

games.title: Goldeneye 007
games.description: GoldenEye 007 is a 1997 first-person shooter game developed by Rare and published by Nintendo for the Nintendo 64. It is based on the 1995 James Bond film GoldenEye, with the player controlling the secret agent James Bond to prevent a criminal syndicate from using a satellite weapon. They navigate a series of levels to complete objectives, such as recovering or destroying objects, while shooting enemies. In a multiplayer mode, up to four players compete in several deathmatch scenarios via split-screen.

games.title: Rush 2 (n64)
games.description: Rush 2: Extreme Racing USA is a 1998 racing video game developed by Atari Games and published by Midway exclusively for the Nintendo 64. It is the second game in the Rush series. The game is notable for the high level of detail in the recreations of the various cities and states used, and for its fast arcade-style physics. The game features a two-player mode and Rumble Pak support. Hidden shortcuts and jumps add to the replay value of the game. Mountain Dew soda cans appear in the game and can be collected to unlock content.

games.title: Soul Calibur 2
games.description: Soulcalibur II is a 2002 fighting game developed and published by Namco. It is the third installment in the Soulcalibur series of weapon-based fighting games as the sequel to Soulcalibur (1998). Soulcalibur II includes the same modes of play as most fighting games: Arcade, Versus Battle, Team Battle (similar to Arcade, but with teams of up to 3 characters and without cutscenes or endings), Versus Team Battle (with teams of up to 8 characters), Time Attack (where the player has to race against the clock to set records), Survival and Practice.

games.title: Tony Hawk Pro Skater
games.description: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater is a 1999 skateboarding video game developed by Neversoft and published by Activision. It is the first installment in the Tony Hawk's series. It takes place in an urban environment permeated by an ambience of punk rock and ska punk music. The player takes control of a variety of skateboarders and must complete missions by performing skateboarding tricks and collecting objects.

games.title: Jackie Chan Stuntmaster
games.description: Jackie Chan Stuntmaster is a 2000 single player beat 'em up platform game. It was developed by Radical Entertainment and published by Midway Games. The story follows Jackie Chan as he fights through New York City to rescue his kidnapped grandfather. You can perform signature Jackie Chan moves like wall-running, cartwheel kicks, and Drunken Master style. You can also pick up objects like chairs and brooms to use as weapons. Jackie Chan provided motion-capture work and voice acting for the game.

games.title: Jade Cacoon
games.description: Jade Cocoon is a 1998 single and multiplayer RPG. It was developed by Genki and published by Crave Entertainment. The game combines RPG elements with monster capturing and fusion mechanics. You play as Levant, a young man who must become a Cocoon Master to save his village from a curse. You can capture monsters called Minions and fuse them together to create new creatures. The game features character designs by Katsuya Kondō, who worked on Studio Ghibli films.

games.title: Monster Rancher 2
games.description: Monster Rancher 2, is a 1999 life simulation role-playing video game developed and published by Tecmo for the PlayStation. Monster Rancher 2 is a game of breeding, training, and fighting. A monster is "born" by inserting a CD into the console at the requested time. The game reads a portion of data from the CD and creates a unique monster.

games.title: Final Fantasy 9
games.description: Final Fantasy IX is a 2000 role-playing video game developed and published by Square for the PlayStation. The plot focuses on a war between nations in a medieval fantasy world called Gaia. Players follow a thief named Zidane Tribal who kidnaps princess Garnet Til Alexandros XVII as part of a ploy by the neighboring nation of Lindblum. He joins Garnet and a growing cast of characters on a quest to take down her mother, Queen Brahne of Alexandria, who started the war, while uncovering the various reasons behind it.

games.title: Metal Gear 1
games.description: Metal Gear Solid is a 1998 action-adventure stealth game developed and published by Konami for the PlayStation. Players control Solid Snake, a soldier who infiltrates a nuclear weapons facility to neutralize the terrorist threat from FOXHOUND, a renegade special forces unit. Snake must liberate hostages and stop the terrorists from launching a nuclear strike.

games.title: Armored Core 2
games.description: Armored Core 2 is a 2000 third-person shooter mecha video game developed by FromSoftware for the PlayStation 2  in which players pilot machines called Armored Cores. These Cores can be fully customized with different parts, weapons, and paint schemes to create a unique loadout. Players must balance their loadout to ensure that they aren't too heavy or are using too much power. In order to purchase more parts, players must earn credits through missions or the Arena.

games.title: Katamari Damacy
games.description: Katamari Damacy is a 2004 action-puzzle video game developed and published by Namco for the PlayStation 2. The game's plot concerns a diminutive prince on a mission to rebuild stars, constellations, and the Moon, which were inadvertently destroyed by his father, the King of All Cosmos. This is achieved by rolling a magical, highly adhesive ball called a katamari around various locations, collecting increasingly larger objects, ranging from thumbtacks to human beings to mountains, until the ball has grown large enough to become a star.

games.title: Red Faction
games.description: Red Faction is a single & multiplayer 2001 first-person shooter. Developed by Volition and published by THQ, it is the first in the Red Faction series of games. The story takes place on Mars in the year 2075. You play a miner who joins a revolutionary group called the Red Faction. The game is famous for its Geo-Mod technology; this allows you to destroy terrain and structures in real-time. You can do things like blow holes through walls to create new paths.

games.title: GTA Vice City
games.description: Grand Theft Auto: Vice City is a 2002 action-adventure game developed by Rockstar North and published by Rockstar Games. It is the fourth main game in the Grand Theft Auto series, following 2001's Grand Theft Auto 3. Set in 1986 within the fictional Vice City, the single-player story follows gangster Tommy Vercetti's rise to power after being released from prison and becoming caught up in an ambushed drug deal. While seeking out those responsible, Tommy gradually builds a criminal empire by seizing power from other criminal organisations.

games.title: Tony Hawk Underground
games.description: Tony Hawk's Underground is a 2003 skateboarding video game and the fifth entry in the Tony Hawk's series It was developed by Neversoft and published by Activision for the Playstation 2. Unlike its predecessors, this game focuses heavily on the story mode and overarching narrative, including a large number of in-game cutscenes.

games.title: Zone of the Enders 2
games.description: Zone of the Enders: The 2nd Runner is a action hack and slash video game developed and published by Konami for the PlayStation 2 in 2003. The sequel to Zone of the Enders is based around mecha combat with the player once again controlling the "Orbital Frame" Jehuty. The plot is set two years after the events from Zone of the Enders and focuses on Dingo Egret, a former member from Mars' military organization BAHRAM whose leader Ridley "Nohman" Hardiman wants back with him. Nohman shoots Dingo following his rejection, but the agent Ken Marinaris saves Dingo by connecting his body to Jehuty and forces him to work in order to defeat BAHRAM.

games.title: Oni (bungie)
games.description: Oni is a third-person action video game developed by Bungie West, a division of Bungie. It was originally released by Gathering of Developers for Microsoft Windows and Mac OS in 2001; a PlayStation 2 port developed by Rockstar Canada was released the same year. It was Bungie West's only game. Its gameplay consists of third-person shooting with hand-to-hand combat, with a focus on the latter. The game's style was largely inspired by the films Ghost in the Shell (1995) and Akira (1988) and shares the same genre, being set in a cyberpunk world.

games.title: Ragnarok Online
games.description: Ragnarok Online  is a massive multiplayer online role-playing game (MMORPG) created by Gravity based on the manhwa Ragnarok by Lee Myung-jin. It was released on 31 August 2002 for Windows. The gameplay is heavily based around Norse mythology, wherein the characters are taken from the stories around Ragnarök. Player characters interact in a 3D environment but are represented by 2D character sprites for front, back, side and diagonal facings. The major types of server-supported gameplay are Player vs Environment, Guild vs Guild, Player vs Player. Players can sign up for quests and submit them for rewards as well as access storage and teleport services for a small fee. Every town will have the usual stores and shops where players can purchase and sell their items.

games.title: Doom
games.description: Doom is a 1993 first-person shooter game developed and published by id Software for MS-DOS. It is the first installment in the Doom franchise. The player assumes the role of a space marine, later unofficially referred to as Doomguy, fighting through hordes of undead humans and invading demons. The game begins on the moons of Mars and finishes in hell, with the player traversing each level to find its exit or defeat its final boss. It is an early example of 3D graphics in video games, and has enemies and objects as 2D images, a technique sometimes referred to as 2.5D graphics.

games.title: Half-Life
games.description: Half-Life is a 1998 first-person shooter game developed by Valve Corporation and published by Sierra Studios for Windows. It was Valve's debut product and the first game in the Half-Life series. At the underground Black Mesa Research Facility, the theoretical physicist Gordon Freeman participates in an experiment on a crystal of unknown origin. This triggers a "resonance cascade", which greatly damages the facility and teleports in hostile alien creatures through a portal between the Xen and Earth dimensions. Venturing to the surface, Freeman discovers U.S. federal government soldiers have been dispatched to cover up the incident by killing humans and aliens alike. A scientist instructs him to make his way to the Lambda Complex to stop the alien invasion.

games.title: Medal of Honor: Allied Assault
games.description: Medal of Honor: Allied Assault is a 2002 first-person shooter game developed by 2015 and published by Electronic Arts.The player takes on the role of Lt. Mike Powell of the United States Army Rangers who works for the Office of Strategic Services. Single-player missions include assaulting Nazi bases in Algiers and Norway, storming Omaha Beach on D-Day, and rescuing comrades behind enemy lines in occupied France. Multiplayer contains four different modes. Deathmatch in which every player has to kill everyone else and get the highest score, Team Deathmatch in which a team has to kill other teams and get the highest score, Round Based which is similar in gameplay to Deathmatch but counts the rounds instead of the score and "Objective" mode where a player must win the most rounds by completing missions.

games.title: Company of Heroes
games.description: Company of Heroes is a 2006 real-time strategy video game developed by Relic Entertainment. The game  is set during the Second World War and contains two playable factions. Players aim to capture strategic resource sectors located around the map, which they use to build base structures, produce new units, and defeat their enemies. In the single-player campaign the player commands two U.S. military units during the Battle of Normandy and the liberation of France. The game allows multiplayer matches of 1-8 players via LAN or the Internet, with the ability to fight as both the Allied and Axis forces.

games.title: Dark Messiah
games.description: Dark Messiah of Might and Magic is a 2006 first-person action role-playing game developed by Arkane Studios. The player controls Sareth, the apprentice of the wizard Phenrig, after he is sent to the city of Stonehelm to accompany an expedition trying to retrieve a powerful artifact known as "The Skull of Shadows".

games.title: Fallout 2
games.description: Fallout 2 is a 1998 Isometric RPG set in a post-nuclear-apocalyptic wasteland, and a sequel to the first installment. Set in the wasteland, and having suffered the worst drought on record, the player is sent outside the protection of their vault to locate a "G.E.C.K" or Garden of Eden Creation Kit which is a device that can be used to transform a nuclear wasteland into a thriving community. It is developed by Black Isle Studios and published by Interplay Productions.

games.title: Diablo 2
games.description: Diablo II is a 2000 action role-playing game developed by Blizzard North and published by Blizzard Entertainment for PC. Set shortly after the events of Diablo, the player controls a new hero, attempting to stop the destruction unleashed by Diablo's return. The game's four acts feature a variety of locations and settings to explore and battle in, as well as an increased cast of characters to play as and interact with.

games.title: Diablo 2: Median XL (mod)
games.description: Median XL is a 2001 total conversion mod for Diablo 2. The mod completely overhauls the base game with 30 new skills per class. It adds thousands of new items and over 40 endgame quests. The mod features new areas to explore, including Heaven and the realms of Hell. It includes quality of life improvements like automatic gold pickup.

games.title: Starcraft
games.description: StarCraft is a 1998 real-time strategy game. It was developed and published by Blizzard Entertainment. It is a single-player and multiplayer game. The story takes place in a distant galaxy where three factions fight for control: the Terran, the Protoss, and the Zerg. The single-player campaign follows each faction through a connected narrative. The game is known for its balanced asymmetrical factions and deep strategy. It became a cornerstone of esports, especially in South Korea.

games.title: Titan Quest
games.description: Titan Quest is a 2006 single & multiplayer action role-playing game. It was developed by Iron Lore Entertainment and published by THQ. The story takes place in ancient Greece, Egypt, and China. You play a hero who must stop the Titans from escaping their prison. The gameplay is comparable to Diablo, with loot, character classes, and skill trees. You combine two of eight masteries to create a unique class. An expanded edition called Titan Quest: Anniversary Edition was released in 2016.

games.title: Travian
games.description: Traivan is a multiplayer 2004 MMO-RTS browser-based game developed by Travian Games. It is set in classical antiquity, and you chose from three tribes: Romans, Gauls or Teutons. You start the game in a small village and must gather resources to build structures, train armies and attack other players to plunder their resources. It is an online game, so progress continues even when you are logged off.

games.title: Soldat
games.description: Soldat is a 2002 multiplayer 2D action game. It was originally developed by Michał Marcinkowski, who then became CEO of Transhuman Design who later became the developers. It was also published by Transhuman Design. It is often described as a mix of Counter-Strike and Worms, you control a ragdoll soldier in battles. It features several weapons and dozens of maps, and full support for user-created content.

games.title: Starseige Tribes
games.description: Starsiege: Tribes is a 1998 multiplayer first-person shooter. It was developed by Dynamix and published by Sierra Studios. The game is set in a sci-fi universe where you play as an armored warrior, using a jetpack. The game features large, open maps with up to 32 players per match. It includes team-based modes like Capture the Flag and base assault. Players can deploy turrets, sensors, and inventory stations on the battlefield.

games.title: Momentum Missle Mayhem
games.description: Momentum Missle Mayhem is a 2007 Flash game developed by Newgrounds user dz2001. It's a tower defense and physics based game focused on destroying tanks trying to invade your space by pulling back your slingshot-like cannon and launching bullets towards enemies.

games.title: Sim City 2000
games.description: SimCity 2000 is a city-building simulation video game jointly developed by Will Wright and Fred Haslam of Maxis. It is the successor to SimCity Classic (1989) and was released for Apple Macintosh and MS-DOS personal computers in 1993. SimCity 2000 is played from an isometric perspective as opposed to the previous title, which was played from a top-down perspective. The objective of the game is to create a city, develop residential and industrial areas, build infrastructure such as power and water facilities and collect taxes for further development of the city. Importance is put on increasing the standard of living of the population, maintaining a balance between the different sectors, and monitoring the region's environmental situation to prevent the settlement from declining and going bankrupt, as extreme deficit spending gets a game over.

games.title: Roller Coaster Tycoon 2
games.description: RollerCoaster Tycoon 2 is a 2002 construction and management simulation game developed by Chris Sawyer and published by Infogrames Interactive. Released for Windows as the sequel to RollerCoaster Tycoon, the game simulates the management of amusement parks.

games.title: Soldier of Fortune 2
games.description: Soldier of Fortune II: Double Helix is a first-person shooter video game developed by Raven Software, the sequel to Soldier of Fortune. The theme of the Double Helix is germ warfare, as the mercenary Mullins and his new partner Madeline Taylor travel to Colombia to investigate a viral outbreak in a small town, only to link it to a shadowy organization called Prometheus. The virus, called Romulus, is followed by a computer virus called Remus which is programmed to delete files on any computer in the world. Double Helix pushed the boundaries of depictions of gore and violence, and is considered more graphic and realistic than most in the first-person genre.

games.title: Emperor: Battle for Dune
games.description: Emperor: Battle for Dune is a real-time strategy video game, released by Westwood Studios in June 2001. It is based in Frank Herbert's science fiction Dune universe. It follows its predecessors, Dune II and Dune 2000 and is their direct sequel. The player commands one of three Houses and must fight for control of the spice mélange on the planet Arrakis. The player harvests spice, builds a base, and trains units to destroy one or more enemies similar to the gameplay in previous real-time strategy titles produced by Westwood Studios. Each of the three main factions is given a single player campaign with full motion video cut scenes. Multiplayer was also available online, through local area networks, or as skirmish battles against computer opponents.

games.title: Battlefield 1942
games.description: Battlefield 1942 is a 2002 first-person shooter game developed by Digital Illusions CE and published by Electronic Arts. It is the first installment in the Battlefield series. The game can be played in single-player mode against the video game AI or in multiplayer mode against players. It is a popular platform for mod developers, with many released modifications that alter the gameplay and theme. In-game, players assume the role of one of five classes of infantry: Scout, Assault, Anti-Tank, Medic, and Engineer. Players also have the ability to fly various World War II fighter aircraft and bombers, navigate capital ships, submarines, and aircraft carriers, man coastal artillery defenses, drive tanks, APCs and jeeps, and take control of anti-aircraft guns and mounted machine guns.

games.title: Nuclear Throne
games.description: Nuclear Throne is a bullet hell roguelike video game developed by Vlambeer. Set in a post-apocalypse, players are tasked with killing enemies by using various weapons and characters' special abilities to progress through levels and eventually reach the titular Nuclear Throne. The game is widely known for its steep difficulty curve and fast-paced gameplay.  Nuclear Throne was released for Linux, Microsoft Windows, OS X, PlayStation 4, and PlayStation Vita in December 2015, for Nintendo Switch in March 2019, and for Xbox One in September 2021.

games.title: Binding of Isaac
games.description: The Binding of Isaac is a 2011 roguelike action-adventure game designed by independent developers Edmund McMillen and Florian Himsl. The Binding of Isaac is a top-down dungeon crawler game, presented using two-dimensional sprites, in which the player controls Isaac or other unlockable characters as they explore the dungeons located in Isaac's basement. The characters differ in speed, amount of health, amount of damage they deal, and other attributes. On each floor of the basement dungeon, the player must fight monsters in a room before continuing onto the next room. This is most commonly done by the character's tears as bullets in the style of a twin-stick shooter, but the player can also use a limited supply of bombs to damage enemies and clear out parts of the room.

games.title: Slay the Spire
games.description: Slay the Spire is a 2019 roguelike deck-building game developed by the American indie studio Mega Crit.  In Slay the Spire, the player attempts to ascend a spire of multiple floors created through procedural generation as one of four characters, battling enemies and bosses. Combat takes place through a collectible card game-based system, with the player gaining new cards as rewards from combat and other means, requiring the player to employ deck-building strategies to construct an effective deck to complete the climb.

games.title: Wolfenstein
games.description: Wolfenstein is a 2009 first-person shooter game developed by Raven Software and published by Activision, part of the Wolfenstein video game series. It serves as a loose sequel to the 2001 entry Return to Castle Wolfenstein. B.J is sent to Isenstadt to find and retrieve the Thule medallion from a Nazi general on the German battleship Tirpitz to prevent the Nazis from using it.

games.title: Skyrim
games.description: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim is a 2011 action role-playing game developed by Bethesda Game Studios and published by Bethesda Softworks. It is the fifth main installment in The Elder Scrolls series.  The game is set 200 years after the events of Oblivion and takes place in Skyrim, the northernmost province of Tamriel, a continent on the planet Nirn. Its main story focuses on the player character, the Dragonborn, on their quest to defeat Alduin the World-Eater, a dragon prophesied to destroy the world. Throughout the game, the player completes quests and develops the character by improving skills. The game continues the open world tradition of its predecessors.

games.title: Oblivion
games.description: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion is a 2006 action role-playing game developed by Bethesda Game Studios. It is the fourth installment in The Elder Scrolls series, following 2002's The Elder Scrolls III: Morrowind. Taking place within the fictional province of Cyrodiil, the game's main story focuses on the player character's efforts to thwart a fanatical cult known as the Mythic Dawn that plans to open portal gates to a demonic realm known as Oblivion.

games.title: Elden Ring
games.description: Elden Ring is a 2022 action role-playing game developed by FromSoftware and published by Bandai Namco Entertainment. It was directed by Hidetaka Miyazaki with worldbuilding provided by the American fantasy writer George R. R. Martin. It includes elements that are similar to those in other FromSoftware-developed games, such as the Dark Souls series, Bloodborne, and Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice. The biggest change is a free to explore open world, allowing some freedom in order of objectives and exploring optional areas.

games.title: Deadlock
games.description: Deadlock is an upcoming third-person shooter and multiplayer online battle arena game in early access developed and published by Valve. It is set in an alternate urban fantasy New York in the 20th century, featuring battles between two teams of six heroes trying to destroy each other's "Patron" gods as part of an occult ritual.

games.title: Crown Trick
games.description: Crown Trick is a 2020 rogue-like RPG adventure developed by NEXT Studios characterized by turn-based combat and skill-item interactive strategy. Enter a labyrinth that moves as you move, where mastering the elements is key to defeating enemies and uncovering the mysteries of this underground world. The game has procedurally generated mazes and synchronous turn-based strategy where the game moves as the player moves.

games.title: Into the Breach
games.description: Into the Breach is a 2018 turn-based strategy video game developed and published by indie studio Subset Games. The game is set in the far future where humanity fights against an army of giant monsters collectively called the Vek. To combat them, the player controls pilots that operate giant mechs that can be equipped with a variety of weapons, armor, and other equipment. The game uses a turn-based combat system, allowing the player to coordinate the actions of their team in response to enemy moves and actions that serve to telegraph their attacks.

games.title: Druidstone: The Secret of Mana
games.description: Druidstone: The Secret of the Menhir Forest is a story driven, party-scale isometric tactical role-playing game developed and published by Ctrl Alt Ninja. The game takes place in the fantasy world of Elo Sphaera and follows main characters Leonhard, Aava, and Oiko on their journey with a focus on grid-based combat.

games.title: Divinity Original Sin
games.description: Divinity: Original Sin is a 2014 role-playing video game developed and published by Larian Studios, in which players assume the roles of heroes on a quest to rid the world of evil magic. From a ¾-overhead perspective, players traverse landscapes and engage in turn-based combat to kill humans and fantastical creatures. The fourth main entry in the Divinity game series, it is a prequel to the original game Divine Divinity.  The game touts turn-based action and adventure, cooperative multiplayer, an interactive world, and includes a modding tool used for creating new content.

games.title: Divinity Original Sin 2
games.description: Divinity: Original Sin II is a 2017 isometric turn-based tactical role-playing video game by Larian Studios. The sequel to Divinity: Original Sin (2014) and the fifth main entry in the Divinity series. The player controls a "Godwoken", a persecuted user of a powerful magic known as Source, and becomes a pivotal figure in the fight against the Voidwoken, which are monstrous creatures who wreak havoc in the medieval fantasy world of Rivellon.

games.title: Noita
games.description: Noita is a 2020 platformer and roguelike game developed by Nolla Games. Players control a witch that can collect and cast spells with wands in order to defeat enemies named after Finnish mythological creatures. The main game leads the player down a series of pseudorandomly generated areas ending in a boss fight, although the game contains much more secret and supplementary content.

games.title: Ori and the Blind Forest
games.description: Ori and the Blind Forest is a 2015 platform-adventure Metroidvania video game developed by Moon Studios. Players assume control of Ori, a small white spirit, and Sein, the "light and eyes" of the Forest's Spirit Tree. Players are tasked to move between platforms and solve puzzles. The game features a save system called "Soul Links", which allows players to save their progress at will with limited resources, and an upgrade system that allows players to strengthen Ori's skills and abilities.

games.title: Satisfactory
games.description: Satisfactory is a 2024 factory simulation game by Coffee Stain Studios for Windows, Xbox, and PlayStation. The player (referred to as "pioneer") is dropped onto an alien planet with a handful of tools and must use the planet's natural resources to construct increasingly complex factories. Satisfactory was made available for early access on 19 March 2019.

games.title: Strike Vector
games.description: Strike Vector is a 2014 multiplayer first-person shooter video game developed by the French independent studio Ragequit Corporation. Strike Vector is an arena shooter. The players control the Vector, a highly customizable and manoeuvrable jet fighter. Your can instantly switch between 2 movement modes: Jet Mode (high-speed, for intercepting opponents and evading incoming projectiles) and Stationary Mode (allows strafing in all directions with increased precision).

games.title: Super House of Dead Ninjas
games.description: Super House of Dead Ninjas is a platform game developed by Megadev and published by Adult Swim Games for Microsoft Windows in 2013. The game is a remake of House of Dead Ninjas, a freeware browser game available on the Adult Swim website. The player plays as a ninja and is tasked with descending a randomly generated 350-storey tower. Players have a limited time to descend all 350 storeys, with power-ups available to increase the time. The player character fills a "rage" meter by defeating enemies; when full, the player enters into a "rage" mode that makes them deal greater damage and become invulnerable until it runs out.

games.title: Tinertia
games.description: TINERTIA is a 2015 action-platformer developed by Candescent Games and Section Studios set on a dying planet. The main protagonist - Weldon - finds himself trapped on this world. But Weldon doesn’t plan to stick around. He must climb a vast expanse of ruins and tackle deadly sentient machines if he hopes to survive.

games.title: Torchlight: Infinite
games.description: Torchlight: Infinite is a 2023 action RPG dungeon crawler developed and published by Chinese company XD Inc.,  where the player controls a character in a high fantasy world, much like previous installments in the Torchlight series. From an isometric, top-down view, the player controls their character to move about the game's world, using hack and slash with a variety of weapons, magic spells, and skills to fight monsters, collect new items and treasure, and sell and buy items to improve their character's abilities.

games.title: Wizard of Legend
games.description: Wizard of Legend is a top-down 3D indie roguelike video game released by American studio Contingent99 and Humble Bundle in May 2018. Players take control of a wizard as they traverse a procedurally generated dungeon, using a wide variety of spells to defeat mobs, three major bosses, and a final boss to earn the title "Wizard of Legend".

games.title: Terraria
games.description: Terraria is a 2011 action-adventure video game developed by Re-Logic. As a sandbox game, Terraria has no set goals. After creating the player character and choosing the game's difficulty, the player is placed in a two-dimensional, procedurally generated world where they explore, fight enemies, gather resources, and craft equipment. Players beat bosses, a tougher variety of enemies, to gain access to more items, resources, and equipment. By completing select goals, players receive access to non-player characters (NPCs) who sell items and offer services, such as healing and fishing quests. Terraria can be played alone or with others, and features support for modding.

games.title: Loadout
games.description: Loadout is a 2014 free-to-play multiplayer third-person shooter developed by Edge of Reality.  Loadout focused on arcade-style multiplayer firefights across a variety of modes and used exaggerated cartoon gore. Players could alter the appearance of their in-game avatar, as well as the properties of their weapons using in-game currency.

games.title: Nosgoth
games.description: Nosgoth was a free-to-play multiplayer action game, developed by Psyonix and published by Square Enix for Microsoft Windows. The game's open beta began in January 2015 and ended in May 2016, when the game was cancelled and its servers were shut down. Nosgoth was an asymmetrical online multiplayer action game. Gameplay focuses on melee Vampire characters against the ranged Human characters, and consists of two modes: Deathmatch and Flashpoint.

games.title: Rogue Legacy
games.description: Rogue Legacy is a 2013 platform game with roguelike elements developed and published by Cellar Door Games. The goal of Rogue Legacy is to explore a randomly generated dungeon, defeat four bosses in each of the four unique environments of the dungeon, and then defeat the final boss. Characters have the default ability to jump and slash with their sword, along with secondary abilities, such as magic attacks, which use mana. Players can also use their sword to slash platforms to make them extend. Whenever a character dies as a result of losing all of their Hit Points (HP), control will transfer over to one of three randomly generated heirs which the player may choose between, though this number may be increased to six with a later upgrade. Each descendant carries their unique characteristics and abilities.

games.title: Battlefield Bad Company 2
games.description: Battlefield: Bad Company 2 is a 2010 first-person shooter game developed by DICE and published by Electronic Arts. The game is primarily a squad-level first-person shooter based in a contemporary modern warfare setting. Additionally, the game includes a single-player campaign, where the player re-assumes the role of Preston Marlowe, the protagonist of the original game, and his squad as they attempt to stop a Russian super-weapon. The game's Frostbite 1.5 engine allows for destructible environments. Multiplayer maps, which allow for five different game modes, contain a wide selection of vehicles, aircraft and emplacements.

games.title: Orcs Must Die 2
games.description: Orcs Must Die! 2 is a 2012 trap based action-tower defense video game developed by Robot Entertainment as the follow-up to the original Orcs Must Die. Like its predecessor, Orcs Must Die! 2 is a variation on a tower defense game. As either the War Mage or Sorceress in the campaign mode, the player must attempt to defend one or more Rifts (up to 3) from an onslaught of orc armies that emerge from one or more doors in a given level. The orc armies arrive in waves, between which the player is given time to recover and place additional traps.

games.title: Dirty Bomb
games.description: Dirty Bomb, formerly known as Extraction, is a 2015 free to play first-person shooter multiplayer video game developed by Splash Damage. At the beginning of a match, the player is placed on one of two teams, either Jackal or CDA, and must complete various objectives in order to win the round whilst fighting both the opposing team and the clock. The player can choose up to three mercenaries, or "mercs", to play as during the round and can switch between them freely between lives. Teamwork and communication are strongly encouraged during the game, hinted at by the ability to revive 'downed' players, as well as the fact that being the "lone wolf" often leads to a short life.

games.title: Mark of the Ninja
games.description: Mark of the Ninja is a 2012 side-scrolling stealth platform game developed by Klei Entertainment. The character is required to sneak through a series of environments while staying out of sight of guards and performing silent assassinations. Levels are divided into areas of light and darkness; the player character is visible to enemies in the former and invisible in the latter, though passing within a certain immediate distance of an enemy's face results in the player being spotted irrespective of lighting. Sounds such as running may also give away the player character's location and are represented visually by a rapidly expanding circle.

games.title: Life is Strange
games.description: Life Is Strange is a 2015 episodic adventure game developed by Dontnod Entertainment and published by Square Enix. The plot focuses on Max Caulfield, an 18-year-old photography student who discovers that she has the ability to rewind time at any moment, leading her every choice to enact the butterfly effect. She uses this ability to solve the disappearance of a girl named Rachel Amber, and the bizarre tragedies that fall upon the women in her academy. The player's actions can alter the narrative as it unfolds, and the player can in many cases rewind time to choose a different option and thus reshape the story. Fetch quests and making environmental changes represent the forms of puzzle solving in addition to using branching choices for conversation.

games.title: Styx Master of Shadows
games.description: Styx: Master of Shadows is a 2014 stealth video game developed by Cyanide Studios. Styx: Master of Shadows is a stealth game where the player must keep to the shadows to survive. Styx has arcane abilities that assist him in his escapades, including the ability to make himself invisible, and the ability to use amber-vision to detect hidden foes and areas. His ability to clone himself through magic offers opportunities to disrupt the traditional stealth gaming formula.

games.title: Nightreign
games.description: Elden Ring Nightreign is a 2025 roguelike action role-playing game developed by FromSoftware and published by Bandai Namco Entertainment. The game is a spin-off to Elden Ring, using many of its features and gameplay elements, and focuses on roguelike and cooperative mechanics. Players control Nightfarers on a quest to dispel the reign of night. The game is set in a procedurally generated version of Limgrave, now named Limveld, the first open-world area of Elden Ring. While the game has a single-player mode, it is intended to be played cooperatively by teams of three players who collaborate over three in-game days to prepare for the final boss. Nightreign features a shrinking gameplay area which resets after players defeat the minor boss at the end of each in-game day. Every match features a different map of randomized enemies, items, and locations.

games.title: Dark Souls 2
games.description: Dark Souls II is a 2014 action role-playing game developed by FromSoftware. The second installment of the Dark Souls series, it is set in the kingdom of Drangleic and follows an undead traveler searching for a cure to their affliction. Despite the new setting, the presentation and gameplay, along with certain lore connections, remain similar to Dark Souls, with notable differences including further penalty for repeated deaths via a "hollowing" mechanic and changes to the invincibility frame mechanics.

games.title: Dishonored
games.description: Dishonored is a 2012 action-adventure game developed by Arkane Studios and published by Bethesda Softworks.The game is played from a first-person perspective and allows the player to undertake a series of missions in a variety of ways, with an emphasis on player choice. Missions can be completed through stealth, combat, or a combination of both. Exploring each level opens new paths and alternatives for accomplishing mission goals, and it is possible to complete all missions, eliminating all of Corvo's targets, in a non-lethal manner. The story and missions are changed in response to the player's violent actions or lack thereof. Magical abilities and equipment are designed to be combined to create new and varied effects.

games.title: Dishonored 2
games.description: Dishonored 2 is a 2016 first person action-adventure game developed by Arkane Lyon and published by Bethesda Softworks. It is the sequel to 2012's Dishonored. It is an action-adventure game with stealth elements played from a first-person perspective. After playing as Empress Emily Kaldwin during the prologue, players may decide to play either as Emily or as Corvo Attano, the protagonist from Dishonored, the previous game. Side missions unlock alternate methods of assassination, non-lethal approaches and paths to navigate the main mission. Both characters wield a pistol, crossbow, a retractable blade, grenades and mines—all of which are upgradable

games.title: Disco Elysium
games.description: Disco Elysium is a 2019 role-playing game. It was developed by ZA/UM and published by ZA/UM. It is a single-player game. You play a detective who has lost his memory to alcohol and drugs. He must solve a murder in a devastated city. The gameplay focuses entirely on dialogue and skill checks. Your own thoughts act like characters you talk to inside your head.

games.title: Smite
games.description: Smite is a 2014 free-to-play third-person multiplayer online battle arena (MOBA) video game developed and published by Hi-Rez Studios. In Smite, players control a god, goddess or other mythological figure and take part in team-based combat, using their abilities and tactics against other player-controlled gods and non-player-controlled minions. In most gamemodes, victory is achieved by slaying the opposing team's titan before losing their own. The game has multiple player versus player (PVP) modes, 130 playable gods, and has a successful esports scene with multiple tournaments.

games.title: Dragon's Dogma
games.description: Dragon's Dogma is a 2012 action role-playing game. It was developed and published by Capcom. It is a single-player game with a unique online pawn system. The story follows a warrior known as the Arisen whose heart is stolen by a dragon. You must hunt down the dragon and defeat it to reclaim your heart. The game features real-time combat where you can climb on large monsters. A unique "Pawn" system lets you create an AI companion and borrow two more from other players online.

games.title: Dying Light
games.description: Dying Light is a 2015 first-person action survival game set in a post-apocalyptic open world overrun by zombies. It is best known for combining aspects of parkour into its gameplay and environment. Developed by Techland Studios.

games.title: Fallout 3
games.description: Fallout 3 is a 2008 first-person open-world RPG set in a post-nuclear-apocalyptic Washington metropolian area. The player controls a 19 year old character, in search of his father after he mysteriously left their vault. Developed and published by Bethesda after they purchased the IP from Interplay.

games.title: Fallout 4
games.description: Fallout 4 is a 2015 first-person open-world RPG set in a post-nuclear-apocalyptic Boston and surrounding region, referred to in-game as "the Commonwealth".

games.title: Far Cry 3
games.description: Far Cry 3 is a 2012 first-person shooter game developed by Ubisoft Montreal and published by Ubisoft. "It is the third main installment in the Far Cry series after Far Cry 2." The game takes place on the fictional Rook Islands, a tropical archipelago which can be freely explored by players.

games.title: Gotham City Imposters
games.description: Gotham City Impostors was a 2012 first-person shooter. It was developed and published by Warner Bros. Interactive Entertainment. The game was a multiplayer-only shooter set in the Batman universe. You played as unhinged civilians dressing as either Batman or the Joker. Teams of "Bats" and "Jokerz" fight in gritty urban arenas. The game went free-to-play in 2012 but received no updates and the servers were shut down in 2014. It was removed from Steam in 2021 and is no longer obtainable.

games.title: Overwatch
games.description: Overwatch (retroactively referred to as Overwatch 1 & Overwatch Classic) was a 2016 live service multiplayer first-person shooter video game by Blizzard Entertainment. The game was first released for PlayStation 4, Windows, and Xbox One in May 2016. It's an online team-based hero shooter allowing players to choose from a roster of characters across different classes.

games.title: Overwatch 2
games.description: Overwatch 2 is a 2022 first-person hero shooter. It was developed and published by Blizzard Entertainment. The game was originally announced as "Overwatch 2" to distinguish it from the original. In 2023, Blizzard dropped the "2" and renamed it simply "Overwatch." The original Overwatch was then shut down and replaced entirely by this version. The game shifted from 6v6 to 5v5 team battles. It also introduced a free-to-play model with a battle pass system. A planned PvE story mode was largely canceled after poor reception.

games.title: Diablo 4
games.description: Diablo 4 is a 2023 multiplayer action role-playing game. It was developed and published by Blizzard Entertainment. The game takes place 50 years after the events of Diablo 3. You fight to stop the daughter of Mephisto, Lilith, from corrupting the world of Sanctuary. The game features an open-world structure with five distinct regions. It includes a shared online world with other players in towns and world events.

games.title: Blacklight: Retribution
games.description: Blacklight: Retribution is a 2012 first-person shooter. It was developed by Zombie Studios (2012-2015) and then by Hardsuit Labs (2015-2025) and published by Perfect World Entertainment. It is a multiplayer-only game. The game is set in a futuristic cyberpunk city. It is known for its unique "Hyper Reality Visor" (HRV) that lets you see through walls briefly. Players can customize weapons with nearly endless part combinations. The game was free-to-play with cosmetic and convenience microtransactions. Its servers were officially shut down in 2019, but community-run versions exist.

games.title: Omega Strikers
games.description: Omega Strikers is a 2023 action sports game. It was developed and published by Odyssey Interactive. It is a multiplayer-only, free-to-play game. The game is a 3v3 air hockey-style brawler where you knock opponents off the arena and score goals. You choose from a roster of colorful strikers, each with unique abilities. Matches are fast paced, lasting only a few minutes. The game features cross-play between PC, mobile, and consoles.

games.title: Path of Exile
games.description: Path of Exile is a free-to-play 2013 single & multiplayer action role-playing game. It was developed and published by Grinding Gear Games. The story takes place in the dark, gothic continent of Wraeclast. You play as an exile who was banished from the corrupt empire of Oriath. It uses a socket-linked skill gem system instead of traditional character classes. Items have sockets that you can link together to support your active skill gems. The game features character customization with seven base classes. It is often compared to Diablo 2 for its setting and complex mechanics.

games.title: Path of Exile 2
games.description: Path of Exile 2 is a 2024 single & multiplayer action role-playing game. Like its predecessor, it is an isometric action role-playing dungeon crawling video game developed and published by Grinding Gear Games. In story returns to Wraeclast, a dark continent where a sinister corruption is spreading and you must fight to end this threat.

games.title: Ratz Instagib
games.description: Ratz Instagib is a 2016 multiplayer FPS. It was developed and published by Firetail Games. The game is a fast-paced arena shooter where every weapon kills in one shot. You play as a small rodent-like creature in various arenas. The gameplay focuses on rocket jumping, strafing, and quick reflexes. Matches are extremely short, lasting only a few minutes  at most.

games.title: Red Orchestra 2
games.description: Red Orchestra 2 is a 2011 multiplayer first-person shooter. It was developed and published by Tripwire Interactive. The game is set on the Eastern Front of World War II; You play as either Soviet or German soldiers in realistic, large-scale battles. The game features authentic weapon mechanics like bullet drop and wind. It also includes a cover system, suppressive fire mechanics, and realistic damage.

games.title: GTA 4
games.description: Grand Theft Auto IV is a 2008 action-adventure game developed by Rockstar North and published by Rockstar Games. Set in the fictional Liberty City, based on New York City, the single-player story follows Eastern European war veteran Niko Bellic and his attempts to escape his past while under pressure from high-profile criminals. The open world design lets players freely roam Liberty City, consisting of four boroughs across three islands, and the neighbouring state of Alderney, which is based on New Jersey. An online multiplayer mode is also included with the game, allowing up to 32 players to engage in both cooperative and competitive gameplay in a recreation of the single-player setting.

games.title: Titanfall 2
games.description: Titanfall 2 is a 2016 single & multiplayer FPS. It was developed by Respawn Entertainment and published by Electronic Arts. The single-player campaign follows rifleman Jack Cooper and his Titan, BT-7274. You build a unique bond with BT as you fight through the planet Typhon. The gameplay features wall-running, double jumping, and piloting massive mechs. The campaign is known for the "Effect and Cause" time-jumping level. Multiplayer modes include six Titans with unique abilities and pilot classes.

games.title: Tree of Saviour
games.description: Tree of Savior is a 2016 MMORPG, developed by IMC Games and published by IMC Games. While an MMORPG, it does have solo and party play options. The game was created by Kim Hakkyu, the designer of Ragnarok Online. The story is based on traditional Lithuanian culture and mythology. You embark on a journey to search for the goddesses in a world thrown into chaos. The game features over 80 character classes with eight ranks to choose from. It is known for its hand-painted, fairy-tale art style and 2.5D perspective.

games.title: Hunie Pop
games.description: HuniePop is a 2015 singleplayer puzzle & dating-sim hybrid game. It was developed and published by HuniePot. The gameplay is split between dating sim conversations and match-4 puzzle mechanics. You give girls gifts and answer questions to earn "Hunie" points. The puzzle segments involve matching tokens to increase your stats for dates. The game is well known for its very suggestive art and mature themes.

games.title: K-Pop Demon Hunters
games.description: KPop Demon Hunters is a 2025 American animated musical urban fantasy film co-written and directed by Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans. It was produced by Sony Pictures Animation for Netflix and stars the voices of Arden Cho, Ahn Hyo-seop, May Hong, Ji-young Yoo, Yunjin Kim, Daniel Dae Kim, Ken Jeong, and Lee Byung-hun. The story follows a K-pop girl group, Huntrix, who lead double lives as demon hunters. They face off against a rival boy band, the Saja Boys, whose members are secretly demons.

games.title: Mewgenics
games.description: Mewgenics is a 2026 singleplayer tactical role-playing roguelite game. It was developed and published by Edmund McMillen and Tyler Glaiel. The game revolves around breeding cats and sending them on turn-based tactical adventures. These cats then return home to breed and pass their skills and mutations on to future generations.

games.title: Battlefield Heroes
games.description: Battlefield Heroes is a 2009 free to play multiplayer third-person shooter game. It was developed by DICE and Easy Studios and published by Electronic Arts. The game features a cartoonish art style. You choose from three classes: Soldier, Gunner, or Commando, and stick with that class for your character. The core gameplay revolves around two armies at war, the Royal Army and the National Army.

games.title: Inside
games.description: Inside is a 2016 singleplayer puzzle-platformer game. It was developed and published by Playdead. The story follows a young boy in a monochromatic, dystopian world. You sneak through forests, farms, and underground facilities while solving environmental puzzles. An eerie, hostile presence constantly hunts you. The game has no dialogue, text, or cutscenes. The story is told entirely through visuals and atmosphere

games.title: Pillars of Eternity
games.description: Pillars of Eternity is a singleplayer 2015 RPG. It was developed by Obsidian Entertainment and published by Paradox Interactive. The story takes place in the fantasy world of Eora, in a region called the Eastern Reach. You play as a Watcher, a person who can see past lives and souls. You must investigate a mysterious supernatural plague called the Hollowborn crisis. The gameplay is a spiritual successor to classic isometric RPGs like Baldur's Gate and Icewind Dale. It features real-time-with-pause combat, character customization, and party-based adventuring.

games.title: Warframe
games.description: Warframe is a 2013 free-to-play multiplayer action role-playing game. It was developed and published by Digital Extremes. The story takes place in a future where humanity has awakened ancient warrior-servants called Tenno. You pilot powerful bio-mechanical suits called Warframes to fight against the Grineer, Corpus, and Infested. It features procedurally generated levels and a deep modding system for weapons and Warframes. The game has a massive story that unfolds over dozens of quests. You can play entirely solo or in squads of up to four players. The game has received continuous free updates since launch, including major expansions like The Second Dream, The War Within, and The New War.

games.title: White Knuckle
games.description: White Knuckle is a first person roguelite speed-climbing game, developed by Dark Machine Games and released on April 17, 2025. In the game, the player must ascend a megastructure SUB-STRUCTURE 17, using precision movement mechanics and managing resources that help with the climb, such as pitons, rebars, or food that restores your stamina.

games.title: Wolcen: Lords of Mayhem
games.description: Wolcen: Lords of Mayhem is a 2020 singleplayer action role-playing game. It was developed and published by Wolcen Studio. The story takes place in a dark fantasy universe where you play one of the "Children of Heimlock," a member of the Republic's Army of the Purifiers. You and your childhood friends embark on a mission called Operation Dawnbane to retaliate against the Brotherhood of Dawn. The game features procedurally generated maps, no class restrictions, and an active dodge mechanic. You can transform into one of four mighty Aspects of Apocalypse, each with unique skills.

games.title: Team Fortress 2
games.description: Team Fortress 2 (TF2) is a 2007 multiplayer first-person shooter game developed and published by Valve Corporation. It is the sequel to the 1996 Team Fortress mod for Quake and its 1999 remake, Team Fortress Classic. It was released as part of The Orange Box. Players join one of two teams—RED and BLU—and choose one of nine character classes to play as in game modes such as capture the flag and king of the hill.

games.title: Dragon Age Origins
games.description: Dragon Age: Origins is a 2009 singleplayer RPG. It was developed by BioWare and published by Electronic Arts. The story takes place in the dark fantasy kingdom of Ferelden. A demonic army called the Darkspawn is invading from the underground tunnels called the Deep Roads. You play as a Grey Warden, an ancient order of warriors sworn to stop the Blight. The game begins with six different origin stories based on your race and class. Each origin changes how NPCs react to you throughout the entire game.

games.title: Tabula Rasa (mmo)
games.description: Tabula Rasa is a 2007 MMORPG. It was developed by Destination Games and published by NCsoft. The story follows humanity's last stand against a group of aliens called the Bane. You fight for the Allied Free Sentients (AFS) on two planets, Arieki and Foreas. The game blends shooter elements into traditional RPG combat. Servers were shut down in 2009 after lower playercounts than anticipated.

games.title: Tribes Ascend
games.description: Tribes: Ascend is a free to play 2012 multiplayer FPS. It was developed and published by Hi-Rez Studios. The game is set in a sci-fi universe as part of the Tribes series. You use your jetpack to move across the maps, and the game features nine class-based loadouts with different weapons and abilities. Modes include Capture the Flag, Team Deathmatch, Arena, and Capture and Hold. The game entered open beta in February 2012 and was officially released on April 12, 2012. Development was halted in July 2013 but briefly resumed in 2015 before ending in September 2016.

games.title: Quake 4
games.description: Quake 4 is a 2005 single & multiplayer FPS. It was developed by Raven Software and published by Activision. The story takes place directly after the events of Quake 2. You play as Matthew Kane, a marine in the Rhino Squad. You are captured and forcibly "Stroggified," partially turned into a cyborg enemy. The game blends fast-paced shooting with vehicle sections and squad-based combat. The multiplayer modes were developed in part by id Software and based on Quake 3 Arena's engine.

games.title: Minecraft
games.description: Minecraft is a sandbox game developed and published by Mojang Studios. Formally released on 18 November 2011 for personal computers following its initial public alpha release on 17 May 2009. In Minecraft, players explore a procedurally generated, three-dimensional world with virtually infinite terrain made up of voxels (cubes). Players can discover and extract raw materials, craft tools and items, and build structures, earthworks, and machines. Depending on the game mode, players can fight hostile mobs, as well as cooperate with or compete against other players in multiplayer. The game's large community offers a wide variety of user-generated content, such as modifications, servers, player skins, texture packs, and custom maps, which add new game mechanics and possibilities.

games.title: Paladins
games.description: Paladins: Champions of the Realm is a 2018 free-to-play online hero shooter video game by Hi-Rez Studios. Paladins is a team-based first-person shooter that focuses on two groups of five players each, who compete for combat-based goals. The game is set in colorful sci-fi fantasy maps, where each player chooses a champion that cannot be repeated in the same team and cannot be changed in the match. Each champion has their own weapons, skills and fighting style; in addition, they can be enhanced and personalized from a card and skin system. The interface of each player is customizable, with avatars and customizable borders to symbolize the player's profile; titles when reaching mastery level 30 with a champion, among others.

games.title: Tactical Intervention
games.description: Tactical Intervention was a multiplayer first-person shooter in which a team of counter-terrorists face off against a team of terrorists, much like Counter-Strike. The game featured dogs, riot shields and the ability to rappel.

games.title: Day of Defeat
games.description: Day of Defeat is a class-based multiplayer first-person shooter video game set in the European theatre of World War II on the Western front. Originally a modification of the 1998 game Half-Life, the rights of the modification were purchased by Valve and released as a full retail title in 2003. Set in the midst of World War II, Day of Defeat includes no single-player campaign, with focus left only on the game's multiplayer aspects. The game favors teamwork and features objective-based gameplay in combination with its system of classes. Maps are primarily made up of narrow paths, all of which typically lead to a few key locations. An official remake of the game, Day of Defeat: Source, was released by Valve in 2005.

games.title: Call of Duty MW2
games.description: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2 is a 2009 first-person shooter game developed by Infinity Ward and published by Activision. The game's campaign follows Task Force 141, a multinational special forces unit commanded by Captain Soap MacTavish as they hunt Vladimir Makarov, leader of the Russian Ultranationalist party, and United States Army Rangers from the 1st Ranger Battalion who are defending the Washington, D.C. area from a Russian invasion. The game was also subject to a controversy surrounding a playable level that had the player participate in a terrorist attack on an airport.

games.title: Call of Duty 4
games.description: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare is a 2007 first-person shooter game developed by Infinity Ward and published by Activision. The game breaks away from the World War II setting of previous entries and is instead set in modern times. The game features a singleplayer campaign mode, focusing on Sergeant "Soap" MacTavish, a recruit to the 22nd SAS Regiment, inducted into a team led by Captain Price and his second-in-command "Gaz". Two main conflicts the story is based on are Russian ultranationalists seeking to restore Soviet-era glory, and a Middle Eastern separatist group taking over a country via a coup. The multiplayer portion of the game features numerous game modes and contains a leveling system that allows the player to unlock additional weapons, weapon attachments, and camouflage schemes as they advance.

games.title: Shootmania Storm
games.description: ShootMania Storm is a first-person shooter video game by Ubisoft and Ubisoft Nadeo as a spin-off title of the TrackMania series. Like the TrackMania games, ShootMania features simplified controls and an in-game level editor that uses building blocks for easy level building. It was heavily inspired by classic arena shooters, including moves like rocket jump.

games.title: Magicka
games.description: Magicka is an action-adventure game developed by Arrowhead Game Studios. It was released via Steam for Microsoft Windows on January 25, 2011. Up to four mages of a sacred order travel to fight against an evil sorcerer and his creations. The game world is based loosely on Norse mythology, but in expansions, the game also focuses on the Vietnam War. The game contains eight base elements (water, life, shield, cold, lightning, arcane, earth and fire) which can be mixed and combined to cast spells. Up to five elements can be chained together for this purpose, but certain elements cannot be combined together due to their opposing nature (for instance, fire and cold). Additionally, two other elements can be created by mixing two base elements: steam (water and fire) and ice (water and cold); these behave like base elements, that gives players access to ten elements to cast spells from.

games.title: Go Go Nippon
games.description: Go! Go! Nippon!: My First Trip to Japan is a 2011 visual novel game developed by Overdrive. The game features a male tourist excited to travel to Japan for the first time with the intention of visiting two male friends. On arriving, he finds out his friends Makoto and Akira are actually two women he is set to stay with for a week. During his visit, the two sisters act as tour guides of cities and sightseeing locations around Japan and offer details about their history. Depending on the choices made in the game, the player has the opportunity to fall in love with one of the girls.

games.title: The Sims 4
games.description: The Sims 4 is a social simulation game developed by Maxis and published by Electronic Arts. As with previous games in the series, The Sims 4 allows players to create and customize characters called "Sims", build and furnish their homes, and simulate their daily life across various in-game regions. The game is open-ended, with no primary objectives to achieve or storyline to complete, focusing instead on gameplay through creativity and experimentation. Players follow and manage the lives of virtual people called "Sims", directing their actions, attending to their "needs", and helping them reach personal goals and desires. Each Sim has a lifelong aspiration, and dynamically generated "wants and fears" goals based on current gameplay.

games.title: Fable
games.description: Fable is an action role-playing video game, the first in the Fable series. It was released in September 2004. Taking place in a fantasy world called Albion, players control an unnamed hero who joins the Heroes Guild after losing his family during a bandit attack on his hometown. Upon leaving the guild, the player is given a choice to decide whether the hero should live up to his name, or guide him on a path of darkness.

games.title: Halo 2
games.description: Halo 2 is a 2004 first-person shooter video game developed by Bungie and published by Microsoft Game Studios. In Halo 2's story mode, the player assumes the roles of the human supersoldier Master Chief and disgraced alien commander Arbiter in a 26th-century conflict between the United Nations Space Command, the genocidal Covenant, and later, the parasitic Flood. Like Halo: Combat Evolved, the Xbox version of Halo 2 features a multiplayer system that allows players to compete with each other in split-screen and system link modes; in addition, it added support for online multiplayer via Xbox Live.

games.title: Ultrakill
games.description: Ultrakill is an upcoming first-person shooter game developed by Arsi "Hakita" Patala and published by New Blood Interactive. It was released on Steam through early access for Microsoft Windows on 3 September 2020. Ultrakill is a fast-paced first-person shooter with an emphasis on movement and stylish techniques. The player must make their way through an interpretation of Dante's layers of Hell, with three acts each made up of three layers being divided into multiple levels. As of February 2026, only Act I and Act II have been fully released, with only the first two layers of Act III being available.

games.title: Bloodborne
games.description: Bloodborne is a 2015 action role-playing game developed by FromSoftware and published by Sony Computer Entertainment for the PlayStation 4. The game follows a Hunter through the decrepit Gothic, Victorian-era inspired city of Yharnam, whose inhabitants are afflicted with a blood-borne disease which transforms the residents into horrific beasts. It's an action role-playing game played from a third-person perspective and features elements similar to those found in the Dark Souls series, also made by FromSoftware. The player makes their way through different locations within the decrepit Gothic city of Yharnam while battling varied enemies, including bosses, collecting different types of items that have many uses, interacting with non-player characters, opening up shortcuts, and continuing through the main story.

games.title: Helldivers 2
games.description: Helldivers 2 is a 2024 cooperative multiplayer PvE third-person shooter video game developed by Arrowhead Game Studios. The sequel to Helldivers (2015), the game is set in the 22nd century and follows the Helldiver Corps of Super Earth, a division of shock troops dispatched to combat various threats to humanity and to spread managed democracy.

games.title: Soul Calibur 5
games.description: Soulcalibur V is a 2012 fighting game developed by Project Soul and published by Namco Bandai Games for the PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360. As the sixth main installment in Namco's Soulcalibur series, Soulcalibur V serves as a follow-up to Soulcalibur IV (2008). It retains the weapon-based combat that characterizes the series while introducing a new protagonist, Patroklos, who aims to free his sister from a curse inflicted upon her by an ancient weapon.

games.title: Soul Calibur 6
games.description: Soulcalibur VI is a 2018 fighting game developed by Bandai Namco Studios and Dimps, and published by Bandai Namco Entertainment. As the seventh main installment and a reboot of the Soulcalibur series, it was released for the PlayStation 4, Windows, and Xbox One. Players engage in combat as they select from a diverse roster of characters, including both returning favorites and new additions. Soulcalibur VI retains classic mechanics like the 8-Way Run and Guard Impact while introducing innovative gameplay features such as Reversal Edge and a revamped Soul Charge system.

games.title: Earth Defense Force 4
games.description: Earth Defense Force 2025 is a third-person shooter developed by Sandlot and published by D3 Publisher, for the PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360. It is the follow-up to Earth Defense Force 2017. A remastered version, titled Earth Defense Force 4.1: The Shadow of New Despair, was released for PlayStation 4 and Windows in 2016. EDF 4.1 is a third-person shooter. The player takes on the role of a member of the EDF's elite Storm Team, selecting from one of four classes detailed above and using a huge range of weaponry to defeat giant insects and alien robots in a series of missions.

games.title: Guilty Gear Strive
games.description: Guilty Gear Strive is a 2021 single & multiplayer fighting game. It was developed and published by Arc System Works. The game features 2.5D graphics and a heavy metal soundtrack. The gameplay includes mechanics like a "Wall Break" system. It is designed to be welcoming for newcomers while remaining deep for veterans. The game has strong online play with rollback netcode.

games.title: Nioh 2
games.description: Nioh 2 is a 2020 action role-playing game. It was developed by Team Ninja and published by Koei Tecmo (with Sony Interactive Entertainment publishing in the West). It is a single-player game with optional online co-op. The game is a prequel to the original Nioh, set in late 1500s Japan. You create your own character, a half-human, half-yokai warrior called a "Hide." The story follows your rise as a wandering mercenary who befriends the historical figure Toyotomi Hideyoshi. The gameplay is known for punishing difficulty, deep combat with multiple stances, and a "Ki Pulse" mechanic for stamina management.

games.title: Forza 6 Horizons
games.description: Forza Horizon 6 is a 2026 open-world racing game. It was developed by Playground Games and published by Xbox Game Studios. It is a single-player game with online coop and multiplayer modes. The game takes place in Japan, featuring Tokyo as the largest urban area in the series. You start as a tourist dreaming of attending the Horizon Festival. The game has hundreds of real-world cars, including classic JDM vehicles.

games.title: Street Fighter 4
games.description: Street Fighter IV is a 2008 single & multiplayer fighting game. It was developed and published by Capcom. The story takes place between Street Fighter II and Street Fighter III. You fight as one of many world warriors in a global martial arts tournament. It introduced new mechanics like Focus Attacks, Revenge Gauge, and Ultra Combos.

games.title: Mass Effect 2
games.description: Mass Effect 2 is a 2010 action role-playing game developed by BioWare and published by Microsoft Game Studios and Electronic Arts. It is the second installment in the Mass Effect series and a sequel to the original Mass Effect. The game takes place within the Milky Way galaxy during the 22nd century, where humanity is threatened by an insectoid alien race known as the Collectors. The player assumes the role of Commander Shepard, an elite human soldier who must assemble and gain the loyalty of a diverse team to stop the Collectors in a suicide mission. Using a completed saved game of its predecessor, the player can impact the game's story in numerous ways. For the game, BioWare changed several gameplay elements and further emphasized third-person shooter aspects, including limited ammunition and regenerable health. In contrast to the exclusive focus on the main story of the original Mass Effect, the developers opted to create a plot where optional missions had as much intensity as the main mission.

games.title: NFL Blitz
games.description: NFL Blitz is a sports game developed by EA Sports released in 2012 for PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360, featuring the teams of the National Football League. It is a reboot of the NFL Blitz series and the first Blitz game officially sponsored by the NFL. NFL Blitz uses seven-on-seven-man American football teams. It also diverges from traditional rules of American football in that there are no penalties, no substitutions, and players can perform illegal attacks on each other such as dropkicks.

games.title: Eleven Table Tennis
games.description: Eleven Table Tennis is a 2016 virtual reality (VR) table tennis game developed by For Fun Labs, Inc. described as a "super realistic table tennis simulator". There are multiple game modes such as singleplayer, multiplayer, and practice mode; as well as minigames such as beer pong and wall tennis.

games.title: Q Remastered
games.description: Q Remastered is a 2023 unique puzzle game developed by liica, Inc. that allows the player to draw various shapes that fall to the ground due to the forces of gravity. The drawn objects are used to solve more than 1200 missions that use physics and have multiple solutions depending on the creativity of the player.

games.title: Beat Saber
games.description: Beat Saber is a 2019 virtual reality rhythm game developed by Beat Games. The player uses VR controllers to relocate a pair of glowing sabers, which by default are colored red and blue for left and right respectively, although the colors are changeable to any color. In each song, the game presents the player with a stream of approaching blocks laid out in sync with the song's beats and notes, located in one of the 12 possible positions of a 4x3 grid.

games.title: Card Crawl
games.description: Card Crawl is a solitaire-style dungeon crawler game developed by Arnold Rauers for iOS, Android and PC, released on 2015. The player has to clear the dungeon of 54 cards by using items, slaying monsters and managing the limited inventory. On each run, you can use five ability cards to gain unique skills.

games.title: The Grid
games.description: The Grid is a 2000 arcade game by Midway. It is a third-person shooter which consists of a super-violent television game show hosted by a Smash TV-like host. The game centres around deathmatches. Players control their character using a joystick with a trigger for shooting, a top button for jumping and trackball for aiming. The object of each match is to accumulate the most points by eliminating other opponents. Local multiplayer with up to six players is possible by linking up multiple cabinets.

games.title: Alien Storm
games.description: Alien Storm is a 1990 singleplayer beat-em-up and shooter game, although multiplayer is possible in the arcade version. The game follows the "Alien Busters," a team consisting of Karen/Karla, Garth/Gordon, and Scooter, who must save Earth from an alien invasion.
